description: shadowProtocol is a WebSocket-gatewayed cyber range dashboard with a strict top-down topology: the React SPA communicates exclusively through a single WebSocket connection to the Bun BFF server, which acts as a stateless session manager. The BFF bifurcates incoming traffic into two planes — a control plane (request/response operations dispatched to Range Management or Caldera) and an observability plane (1-second polling loop that pushes Ludus status and technique data to subscribed frontend clients). The RDP Proxy is a parallel WebSocket upgrade path for browser-based remote desktop access to lab VMs. The frontend is organized into domain-specific workspaces (Attack Configuration, Lab Range) that share a common shell providing the WebSocket client, design system, and utility hooks.

components.name: WebSocket Gateway & Server Core
components.description: Central BFF (Backend-for-Frontend) — a thin Bun server that owns all WebSocket lifecycle, request routing, and the 1-second polling loop that fans Ludus and Caldera state to subscribed frontend clients. All frontend-to-backend communication flows through this single entry point.

components.name: Range Lifecycle Operations
components.description: Core VM orchestration — deploys, deletes, snapshots, and runs Ansible scripts on range VMs. All 32 functions live in server/ludus/range.js.

components.name: Template Management
components.description: Manages VM template YAML definitions — fetching, building, and destroying. Templates must exist before VMs can be deployed.

components.name: Attack Planning UI
components.description: Visual attack configuration surface — ability detail panel with copy-to-execute commands, MITRE ATT&CK technique tree browser, and selected-attack state management.

components.name: AI Chat Engine
components.description: OpenCode SDK–powered conversational assistant — handles message streaming, question answering tool invocations, generation stop, and real-time stream processing for attack planning.

components.name: Tree View Primitive
components.description: Reusable recursive tree component with expand/collapse and selection state — used by TechniqueTree to render the MITRE ATT&CK taxonomy. Part of the kibo-ui design system library.

components.name: Range Visual Components
components.description: Interactive visual UI for lab range topology — YAML code editor with syntax highlighting, deployment progress timeline with step-by-step status, and xyflow VM network topology graph.

components.name: Deployment State Management
components.description: Manages active VM deployment state by subscribing to real-time Ansible play recap data pushed from the server. Parses play output to determine per-VM deployment progress and updates the timeline.

components.name: Range Management Core
components.description: All server/range.js functions — VM lifecycle (deploy, delete, snapshot), range configuration, Ansible execution, and inventory management.

components.name: Deployment & Config Engine
components.description: Core VM lifecycle — deploys VMs, manages range configuration, generates YAML config files, and fetches deployment status with log parsing. Includes conflict detection and IP octet calculation.

components.name: Golden Image Preparation
components.description: Manages golden image pipeline: VM readiness checks, snapshot creation/verification, inventory fetching, connectivity monitoring, base-clean restore.

components.name: Attack Technique Registry
components.description: Provides MITRE ATT&CK technique data for snapshot validation; verifies Caldera connectivity during image preparation.

components.name: Snapshot Validation Utilities
components.description: Test scripts exercising snapshot create/save/reset for manual verification.

components.name: Template Build Status
components.description: Parses Ansible output for in-progress template builds, complementary to snapshot pipeline's log parsing.

components.name: Deployment Readiness UI
components.description: Frontend hook computing golden image readiness — determines if router/Kali/Windows snapshots exist before deployment.

components.name: Deployment Pipeline Hook
components.description: Central useDeploymentPipeline hook — subscribes to backend WebSocket for Ansible play recap data, parses per-VM deployment status using parsePlayRecap, computes timelineItems with setTimelineItemsLocal, and exposes isVmPresent helper.

components.name: Ansible Recap Parser
components.description: Parses per-VM deployment progress from Ansible play recap lines pushed over WebSocket. Extracts VM presence via isVmPresent and converts raw play recap data into structured timeline entries.

components.name: Deployment Timeline State
components.description: Manages WebSocket subscription lifecycle and debounced timeline item computation. Receives backend push data, computes timeline items via setTimelineItemsLocal, and exposes them to the interactive timeline UI.

components.description: Processes incoming WebSocket messages in the polling engine, handling promise resolution and errors for the data stream that feeds the deployment pipeline.

components.name: Caldera Adversary Simulation Engine
components.description: Integrates with MITRE Caldera for automated adversary emulation. Manages attack abilities (local SQLite CRUD), fetches MITRE ATT&CK categories and techniques, and generates focused training data. Powers the attack configuration workspace.

components.name: Technique & Category Service
components.description: Fetches MITRE ATT&CK categories and techniques from the Caldera API. Serves as the primary data source for the attack configuration workspace's technique tree.

components.name: Ability Pipeline
components.description: Generates focused training data by fetching abilities from both Caldera API and local SQLite, then deduplicating, filtering by executor type (PowerShell/cmd), and enriching with payload metadata.

components.name: Custom Ability Store
components.description: Local SQLite-backed CRUD for custom attack abilities. Users can create, read, update, and delete custom abilities that supplement the Caldera-provided ones.

components.name: RDP WebSocket Handler
components.description: Entry point activated by the Bun server when a WebSocket upgrade has isRdp: true. Parses the initial RDCleanPath protocol request, builds certificate/error responses, and orchestrates the tunnel.

components.name: TLS Tunnel & DER Codec
components.description: Performs TLS handshake with the target VM, encodes/decodes ASN.1 DER structures for RDP protocol fields, and establishes the full-duplex TCP-to-WebSocket relay.

components.name: App Shell & Page Router
components.description: React entry point with BrowserRouter, route definitions (main app, prototype UI, preview UI), and top-level ThemeProvider wrapper. Provides the structural skeleton for the entire SPA.

components.name: UI Design System & Theme
components.description: Complete shadcn/ui library with 40+ primitives, kibo-ui (code-block with Shiki highlighting, recursive tree), apple-dock, tab-content-card, and theme provider with dark/light mode transitions.

components.name: Chat & Message Infrastructure
components.description: Reusable chat widget system — renders AI messages with syntax-highlighted Markdown, tool calls, reasoning blocks, and file attachments. Provides voice-enabled text input with file upload and interactive question/answer rendering.

components.name: WebSocket Client
components.description: Singleton WebSocket connection manager maintaining a persistent connection to the Bun BFF server with automatic reconnection, message queuing during disconnection, and pub/sub handler dispatch.

components.name: Application Hooks
components.description: Core React hooks — opencode AI chat lifecycle (SSE streaming, message finalization, tool invocation), clipboard access, and focused data subscriptions from the backend.

components.name: Feature Integration Components
components.description: Higher-level workspace UIs that mount inside the shell — landing tabs with completion gating, attack configuration (technique tree + AI chat + ability tabs), lab range deployment (state, health, topology), knowledge graph, leaderboard, benchmark, SIFT agent, SNR, and settings page.

components.name: RDP Proxy Service
components.description: Tunnels Remote Desktop Protocol traffic from the browser to lab VMs via WebSocket-to-TCP proxying. Performs TLS negotiation and DER encoding/decoding of RDP protocol structures.

components.name: Technique & Category Service
components.description: Fetches MITRE ATT&CK categories and techniques from the Caldera API. Serves as the primary data source for the attack configuration workspace's technique tree.

components.name: Ability Pipeline
components.description: Generates focused training data by fetching abilities from both Caldera API and local SQLite, then deduplicating, filtering by executor type (PowerShell/cmd), and enriching with payload metadata.

components.name: Custom Ability Store
components.description: Local SQLite-backed CRUD for custom attack abilities. Users can create, read, update, and delete custom abilities that supplement the Caldera-provided ones.

components.name: RDP WebSocket Handler
components.description: Entry point activated by the Bun server when a WebSocket upgrade has isRdp: true. Parses the initial RDCleanPath protocol request, builds certificate/error responses, and orchestrates the tunnel.

components.name: TLS Tunnel & DER Codec
components.description: Performs TLS handshake with the target VM, encodes/decodes ASN.1 DER structures for RDP protocol fields, and establishes the full-duplex TCP-to-WebSocket relay.

components.name: App Shell & Page Router
components.description: React entry point with BrowserRouter, route definitions (main app, prototype UI, preview UI), and top-level ThemeProvider wrapper. Provides the structural skeleton for the entire SPA.

components.name: UI Design System & Theme
components.description: Complete shadcn/ui library with 40+ primitives, kibo-ui (code-block with Shiki highlighting, recursive tree), apple-dock, tab-content-card, and theme provider with dark/light mode transitions.

components.name: Chat & Message Infrastructure
components.description: Reusable chat widget system — renders AI messages with syntax-highlighted Markdown, tool calls, reasoning blocks, and file attachments. Provides voice-enabled text input with file upload and interactive question/answer rendering.

components.name: WebSocket Client
components.description: Singleton WebSocket connection manager maintaining a persistent connection to the Bun BFF server with automatic reconnection, message queuing during disconnection, and pub/sub handler dispatch.

components.name: Application Hooks
components.description: Core React hooks — opencode AI chat lifecycle (SSE streaming, message finalization, tool invocation), clipboard access, and focused data subscriptions from the backend.

components.name: Feature Integration Components
components.description: Higher-level workspace UIs that mount inside the shell — landing tabs with completion gating, attack configuration (technique tree + AI chat + ability tabs), lab range deployment (state, health, topology), knowledge graph, leaderboard, benchmark, SIFT agent, SNR, and settings page.

components.name: Frontend Application Shell
components.description: Provides the React SPA skeleton — page routing, all shadcn/ui design system components, theme management, WebSocket client library, and browser utility hooks (audio capture, clipboard). Every feature workspace mounts inside this shell.

components.name: Technique & Category Service
components.description: Fetches MITRE ATT&CK categories and techniques from the Caldera API. Serves as the primary data source for the attack configuration workspace's technique tree.

components.name: Ability Pipeline
components.description: Generates focused training data by fetching abilities from both Caldera API and local SQLite, then deduplicating, filtering by executor type (PowerShell/cmd), and enriching with payload metadata.

components.name: Custom Ability Store
components.description: Local SQLite-backed CRUD for custom attack abilities. Users can create, read, update, and delete custom abilities that supplement the Caldera-provided ones.

components.name: RDP WebSocket Handler
components.description: Entry point activated by the Bun server when a WebSocket upgrade has isRdp: true. Parses the initial RDCleanPath protocol request, builds certificate/error responses, and orchestrates the tunnel.

components.name: TLS Tunnel & DER Codec
components.description: Performs TLS handshake with the target VM, encodes/decodes ASN.1 DER structures for RDP protocol fields, and establishes the full-duplex TCP-to-WebSocket relay.

components.name: App Shell & Page Router
components.description: React entry point with BrowserRouter, route definitions (main app, prototype UI, preview UI), and top-level ThemeProvider wrapper. Provides the structural skeleton for the entire SPA.

components.name: UI Design System & Theme
components.description: Complete shadcn/ui library with 40+ primitives, kibo-ui (code-block with Shiki highlighting, recursive tree), apple-dock, tab-content-card, and theme provider with dark/light mode transitions.

components.name: Chat & Message Infrastructure
components.description: Reusable chat widget system — renders AI messages with syntax-highlighted Markdown, tool calls, reasoning blocks, and file attachments. Provides voice-enabled text input with file upload and interactive question/answer rendering.

components.name: WebSocket Client
components.description: Singleton WebSocket connection manager maintaining a persistent connection to the Bun BFF server with automatic reconnection, message queuing during disconnection, and pub/sub handler dispatch.

components.name: Application Hooks
components.description: Core React hooks — opencode AI chat lifecycle (SSE streaming, message finalization, tool invocation), clipboard access, and focused data subscriptions from the backend.

components.name: Feature Integration Components
components.description: Higher-level workspace UIs that mount inside the shell — landing tabs with completion gating, attack configuration (technique tree + AI chat + ability tabs), lab range deployment (state, health, topology), knowledge graph, leaderboard, benchmark, SIFT agent, SNR, and settings page.

components.name: Attack Configuration Workspace
components.description: Delivers the interactive attack planning environment — browse MITRE ATT&CK technique trees, inspect ability details, and plan attacks via an AI chat assistant powered by the opencode SDK.

components.name: Range Lifecycle Operations
components.description: Core VM orchestration — deploys, deletes, snapshots, and runs Ansible scripts on range VMs. All 32 functions live in server/ludus/range.js.

components.name: Template Management
components.description: Manages VM template YAML definitions — fetching, building, and destroying. Templates must exist before VMs can be deployed.

components.name: Attack Planning UI
components.description: Visual attack configuration surface — ability detail panel with copy-to-execute commands, MITRE ATT&CK technique tree browser, and selected-attack state management.

components.name: AI Chat Engine
components.description: OpenCode SDK–powered conversational assistant — handles message streaming, question answering tool invocations, generation stop, and real-time stream processing for attack planning.

components.name: Tree View Primitive
components.description: Reusable recursive tree component with expand/collapse and selection state — used by TechniqueTree to render the MITRE ATT&CK taxonomy. Part of the kibo-ui design system library.

components.name: Range Visual Components
components.description: Interactive visual UI for lab range topology — YAML code editor with syntax highlighting, deployment progress timeline with step-by-step status, and xyflow VM network topology graph.

components.name: Core State Hook
components.description: The central useLabRangeState hook's core state — handles golden image save/revert/reset actions and manages the templateItems memoized computation.

components.name: Ansible Play Recap Subscriber
components.description: Subscribes to backendWs for real-time fetchRangeWithLog push data. Receives raw Ansible play recap lines pushed by the 1-second polling engine and parses per-VM status using parsePlayRecap.

components.name: Readiness State Engine
components.description: The reactive data pipeline that receives WebSocket push data, filters relevant items, computes structured timeline entries, and evaluates allOk — the golden image readiness check. Diffs previous vs new state and debounces final updates.

components.name: Readiness Action Handlers
components.description: User-initiated deployment state actions. handleReset tears down subscriptions, handleRevert rolls back to a saved snapshot, handleSave persists the current state. templateItems.useMemo provides computed timeline data for the UI layer.

components_relations.relation: Provides processed timeline state and allOk readiness flag

components.name: Timeline Item Computer
components.description: Transforms parsed Ansible recap data into structured timelineItems array with per-VM state (pending/running/complete/failed). Computes derived state like isVmPresent and manages debounced timeouts.

components.name: Debounced State Manager
components.description: Manages setTimeout-based debouncing for timeline items to ensure state stability. Delays the final timeline update until no new data arrives within the debounce window.

components.name: WebSocket Subscription Cleanup
components.description: Manages the subscription lifecycle — subscribes on component mount, unsubscribes on unmount via the cleanup function returned by useEffect(). Prevents memory leaks and stale data.

components.name: Template Status Subscriber
components.description: Subscribes to backend WebSocket for template fetch/build/completion status updates. Receives pushed data from the polling engine's template fetcher and updates internal state on each message.

components.name: Template Build State Tracker
components.description: Tracks per-template build state with internal class containers (allBuilt, allExisted, allItems, firstUnbuiltIdx). Monitors REQUIRED_TEMPLATES and marks each as fetching/building/completed.

components.name: Timeline Item Builder
components.description: Pure function buildTimelineItems that transforms raw template status into structured timelineItems with per-entry status. Iterates over REQUIRED_TEMPLATES and computes completion state using isReallyBuilt.

components.name: Template Readiness Gate
components.description: Exposes the allBuilt boolean that tells parent components whether all required templates are ready. Gates the deployment phase transition from templates to ready.

components.name: Range Lifecycle Phase Manager
components.description: Manages the GatePhase state machine transitioning through gate → templates → ready phases. Determines which phase the range is in based on health check status, template build readiness, and deployment state.

components.name: Golden Image Action Handler
components.description: Handles golden image save/revert/reset actions by sending operations through WebSocket to the backend. Manages handleReset callback and GoldenImageResult interface.

components.name: Template Items Memoized Computer
components.description: Memoized computation of templateItems that transforms raw template status into structured timeline items. Uses useMemo to avoid unnecessary recomputation and marks stale state via isStale.

components.name: WebSocket Subscription Manager
components.description: Manages the backendWs.subscribe() lifecycle — subscribes on mount, processes incoming VM status messages, computes derived state, and unsubscribes on cleanup. Uses setTimeout-based debouncing.

components.name: Deployment Pipeline Hook
components.description: Central useDeploymentPipeline hook — initializes the subscription, manages debounced state updates, and exposes timelineItems and isVmPresent to consumer components.

components.name: Ansible Recap Parser
components.description: Parses raw Ansible play recap log lines into structured per-VM status objects. Uses parsePlayRecap to extract the play recap section and isVmPresent to check VM membership.

components.name: Timeline State Updater
components.description: Updates local timeline state via setTimelineItemsLocal with debounced commits. Computes prev/next diffing via prev.map() and prev.some() to detect meaningful changes.

components.name: WebSocket Subscription Manager
components.description: Manages real-time subscription — connects on mount, processes pushed recap data through the parser and updater pipeline, unsubscribes on unmount cleanup.

components_relations.relation: Raw recap data feeds the timeline computation
Computed timeline items are debounced before commit
Debounce trigger commits the final timeline state
Completion state drives the readiness boolean
Phase state determines which timeline items are shown
Raw push data is passed to the parser
Parsed VM status feeds the timeline update

components.name: Subscription Processing
components.description: Processes real-time WebSocket push data — subscribes to backend status updates, computes timeline items from VM deployment status, and manages debounced timeouts for state stability.

components.name: Ansible Play Recap Subscriber
components.description: Subscribes to backendWs for real-time fetchRangeWithLog push data. Receives raw Ansible play recap lines pushed by the 1-second polling engine and parses per-VM status using parsePlayRecap.

components.name: Deployment Data Backend
components.description: Core backend pipeline that produces Ansible play recap data via fetchRangeWithLog from both direct and Ludus API paths, pushes through polling loop, and broadcasts over WebSocket.

components.name: Pipeline Hook Consumer
components.description: Frontend React hooks that subscribe to the backend WebSocket stream and consume Ansible play recap data. Includes useDeploymentPipeline and useHealthCheck.

components.name: Attack Simulation Backend
components.description: MITRE Caldera adversary emulation integration providing atomic abilities and custom ability management via SQLite.

components.name: RDP Proxy Service
components.description: WebSocket-based RDP proxy that relays RDP traffic through the Bun server.

components.name: Deployment Test Scripts
components.description: Standalone WebSocket test utilities for manual validation of the deployment pipeline's data flow.

components.name: Timeline Diffing
components.description: Diffing logic that compares previous and updated timeline items to detect per-VM state changes. Determines which items have meaningfully changed to avoid unnecessary re-renders.

components.name: Deployment Pipeline Hook
components.description: Central useDeploymentPipeline hook — subscribes to backend WebSocket for Ansible play recap data, parses per-VM status using parsePlayRecap, computes timelineItems via setTimelineItemsLocal, manages debounced timeouts for state stability, and exposes isVmPresent helper.

components.name: Deployment Data Backend
components.description: Core backend pipeline that produces Ansible play recap data via fetchRangeWithLog from both direct and Ludus API paths, pushes through polling loop, and broadcasts over WebSocket.

components.name: Pipeline Hook Consumer
components.description: Frontend React hooks that subscribe to the backend WebSocket stream and consume Ansible play recap data. Includes useDeploymentPipeline and useHealthCheck.

components.name: Attack Simulation Backend
components.description: MITRE Caldera adversary emulation integration providing atomic abilities and custom ability management via SQLite.

components.name: RDP Proxy Service
components.description: WebSocket-based RDP proxy that relays RDP traffic through the Bun server.

components.name: Deployment Test Scripts
components.description: Standalone WebSocket test utilities for manual validation of the deployment pipeline's data flow.

components.name: Deployment State Providers
components.description: Provides snapshot pipeline state (router/Kali/Windows golden image checks) and VM deletion tracking state consumed by the deployment pipeline to determine golden image readiness and detect teardown in progress.

components.name: WebSocket Data Stream
components.description: Processes incoming WebSocket messages in the polling engine, handling promise resolution and errors for the data stream that feeds the deployment pipeline's Ansible recap subscription.

components.name: Test Validation Scripts
components.description: Standalone WebSocket test scripts for manual validation of the subscription processing data flow.

components_relations.relation: Delivers Ansible play recap data via WebSocket push to the hook's subscription
Reads golden image readiness and deletion state before or during deployment

components.name: Deployment State Management
components.description: The useDeploymentPipeline React hook — subscribes to real-time Ansible play recap data pushed from the WebSocket Gateway during active VM deployment, parses per-VM progress, and computes timeline items for Range Visual Components.

components.name: Template Build Readiness
components.description: The useTemplateBuilder React hook — tracks VM template build lifecycle by subscribing to WebSocket push data. Monitors template fetch and build status for each required VM template and determines when all templates are built.

components.name: Range State Management
components.description: Central useLabRangeState hook — manages the range lifecycle phase (gate/templates/ready), subscribes to WebSocket push data for VM status, computes timeline items, and handles golden image save/revert/reset.

components.description: The central useLabRangeState hook's core state — handles golden image save/revert/reset actions and manages the templateItems memoized computation.

components.name: Deployment Pipeline Hook

components.name: Deployment Data Backend
components.description: Core backend pipeline that produces Ansible play recap data via fetchRangeWithLog from both direct and Ludus API paths, pushes through polling loop, and broadcasts over WebSocket.

components.name: Pipeline Hook Consumer
components.description: Frontend React hooks that subscribe to the backend WebSocket stream and consume Ansible play recap data. Includes useDeploymentPipeline and useHealthCheck.

components.name: Attack Simulation Backend
components.description: MITRE Caldera adversary emulation integration providing atomic abilities and custom ability management via SQLite.

components.name: RDP Proxy Service
components.description: WebSocket-based RDP proxy that relays RDP traffic through the Bun server.

components.name: Deployment Test Scripts
components.description: Standalone WebSocket test utilities for manual validation of the deployment pipeline's data flow.

components.name: Deployment State Providers
components.description: Provides snapshot pipeline state (router/Kali/Windows golden image checks) and VM deletion tracking state consumed by the deployment pipeline to determine golden image readiness and detect teardown in progress.

components.name: WebSocket Data Stream
components.description: Processes incoming WebSocket messages in the polling engine, handling promise resolution and errors for the data stream that feeds the deployment pipeline's Ansible recap subscription.

components.name: Test Validation Scripts
components.description: Standalone WebSocket test scripts for manual validation of the subscription processing data flow.

components_relations.relation: Delivers Ansible play recap data via WebSocket push to the hook's subscription
Reads golden image readiness and deletion state before or during deployment

components.name: Timeline Diffing
components.description: Diffing logic that compares previous and updated timeline items to detect per-VM state changes. Determines which items have meaningfully changed to avoid unnecessary re-renders.

components.name: Deployment State Management
components.description: The useDeploymentPipeline React hook — subscribes to real-time Ansible play recap data pushed from the WebSocket Gateway during active VM deployment, parses per-VM progress, and computes timeline items for Range Visual Components.

components.name: Template Build Readiness
components.description: The useTemplateBuilder React hook — tracks VM template build lifecycle by subscribing to WebSocket push data. Monitors template fetch and build status for each required VM template and determines when all templates are built.

components.name: Template Build State
components.description: useTemplateBuilder hook — tracks VM template build readiness (fetching, building, completion) before deployment can proceed.

components.name: Core State Hook
components.description: The central useLabRangeState hook's core state — handles golden image save/revert/reset actions and manages the templateItems memoized computation.

components.name: Subscription Processing
components.description: Processes real-time WebSocket push data — subscribes to backend status updates, computes timeline items from VM deployment status, and manages debounced timeouts for state stability.

components.name: Timeline Diffing
components.description: Diffing logic that compares previous and updated timeline items to detect per-VM state changes. Determines which items have meaningfully changed to avoid unnecessary re-renders.

components.name: Deployment State Management
components.description: The useDeploymentPipeline React hook — subscribes to real-time Ansible play recap data pushed from the WebSocket Gateway during active VM deployment, parses per-VM progress, and computes timeline items for Range Visual Components.

components.name: Template Build Readiness
components.description: The useTemplateBuilder React hook — tracks VM template build lifecycle by subscribing to WebSocket push data. Monitors template fetch and build status for each required VM template and determines when all templates are built.

components.name: Backend Health Monitor
components.description: useHealthCheck hook — monitors WebSocket connectivity to the Bun BFF server and Ludus server health, driving the backend gate UI that blocks range operations when the server is unreachable.

components.name: BFF Server & WebSocket Engine
components.description: The Bun.serve entry point with WebSocket lifecycle (open/message/close), the 1-second polling loop that polls Ludus and fans results to subscribers, health check endpoint, and operation dispatch routing.

components.name: Range Operation Internal State
components.description: Internal class/tracking structures used by the monolithic range.js — VM conflict detectors, snapshot pipeline trackers (check, kali, router, windows), and Ansible log parsers (playLines, recapIdx, statusLine).

components.name: Test Clients
components.description: Standalone WebSocket test scripts (ansibleScriptTest.js, snapshotTest.js) for manual validation of the server's WebSocket API.

Sends user YAML edits back to
Blocks deploy and delete operations of
Is consumed as a prerequisite by
Connects to for manual WebSocket testing

components.name: Lab Range Management UI
components.description: Controls the range deployment lifecycle — YAML topology editor, interactive deployment timeline, VM topology visualization (powered by @xyflow/react), golden image management, and backend health gating that blocks operations when the server is unreachable.

methods: web.src.components.lab-range.use-lab-range-state.useLabRangeState.useEffect() callback.unsub.backendWs.subscribe() callback.timer.setTimeout() callback.setTimelineItems() callback.prev.map() callback.description.prepared.map() callback

components.name: Range Lifecycle Operations
components.description: Core VM orchestration — deploys, deletes, snapshots, and runs Ansible scripts on range VMs. All 32 functions live in server/ludus/range.js.

components.name: Template Management
components.description: Manages VM template YAML definitions — fetching, building, and destroying. Templates must exist before VMs can be deployed.

components.name: Attack Planning UI
components.description: Visual attack configuration surface — ability detail panel with copy-to-execute commands, MITRE ATT&CK technique tree browser, and selected-attack state management.

components.name: AI Chat Engine
components.description: OpenCode SDK–powered conversational assistant — handles message streaming, question answering tool invocations, generation stop, and real-time stream processing for attack planning.

components.name: Tree View Primitive
components.description: Reusable recursive tree component with expand/collapse and selection state — used by TechniqueTree to render the MITRE ATT&CK taxonomy. Part of the kibo-ui design system library.

components.name: Range Visual Components
components.description: Interactive visual UI for lab range topology — YAML code editor with syntax highlighting, deployment progress timeline with step-by-step status, and xyflow VM network topology graph.

components.name: Health Monitor
components.description: The useHealthCheck React hook — monitors WebSocket connectivity to the Bun BFF server and Ludus server health. Drives the backend gate UI that blocks range operations when the server is unreachable.

components.name: Range Deployment Operations
components.description: Core VM lifecycle — deploys VMs, manages range configuration, fetches range status, and handles VM deletion. Cluster 1 and 5 form the complete create/delete lifecycle, both calling apiCall and sleep.

components.name: Snapshot & Golden Image
components.description: Manages VM snapshot lifecycle — creates snapshots, prepares golden images with router/Kali/Windows checks, restores to base clean, and lists snapshots. Depends on Inventory & Connectivity helpers.

components.name: Inventory & Connectivity
components.description: Ansible inventory management, script execution, and VM connectivity monitoring. Fetches inventory, runs Ansible scripts, and waits for VMs to become reachable.

components.name: Helper Functions
components.description: Utility functions — deployment conflict detection and IP octet calculation for VLAN configuration.

components.name: Snapshot Pipeline State Trackers
components.description: Internal tracking state for the prepareGoldenImage snapshot pipeline — tracks which VMs (router, Kali, Windows) have been processed and which snapshot checks have passed.

components.name: Ansible Log Parser State
components.description: State tracking for Ansible play recap log parsing used by fetchRangeWithLog — identifies play recap lines in the raw Ansible output and extracts per-VM execution status.

components.name: Template Log Parsers
components.description: Log parsing state for template fetch operations — identifies in-progress template builds in the Ansible output stream. Parallel implementations exist in server/ludus/templates.js and server/templates.js.

components.name: VM Deletion Trackers
components.description: State tracking for VM deletion operations — tracks which VMs are queued for deletion and their current teardown status.

components_relations.relation: Gates deploy and delete operations of

components.name: Range State Management
components.description: Central useLabRangeState hook — manages the range lifecycle phase (gate/templates/ready), subscribes to WebSocket push data for VM status, computes timeline items, and handles golden image save/revert/reset.

components.name: Golden Image Preparation
components.description: Manages golden image pipeline: VM readiness checks, snapshot creation/verification, inventory fetching, connectivity monitoring, base-clean restore.

components.name: Attack Technique Registry
components.description: Provides MITRE ATT&CK technique data for snapshot validation; verifies Caldera connectivity during image preparation.

components.name: Snapshot Validation Utilities
components.description: Test scripts exercising snapshot create/save/reset for manual verification.

components.name: Template Build Status
components.description: Parses Ansible output for in-progress template builds, complementary to snapshot pipeline's log parsing.

components.name: Deployment Readiness UI
components.description: Frontend hook computing golden image readiness — determines if router/Kali/Windows snapshots exist before deployment.

methods: web.src.components.lab-range.use-lab-range-state.useLabRangeState.useEffect() callback.unsub.backendWs.subscribe() callback.timer.setTimeout() callback.setTimelineItems() callback.prev.map() callback.description.prepared.map() callback

components.name: Readiness State Engine
components.description: The reactive data pipeline that receives WebSocket push data, filters relevant items, computes structured timeline entries, and evaluates allOk — the golden image readiness check. Diffs previous vs new state and debounces final updates.

methods: web.src.components.lab-range.use-lab-range-state.useLabRangeState.useEffect() callback.unsub.backendWs.subscribe() callback.timer.setTimeout() callback.setTimelineItems() callback.prev.map() callback.description.prepared.map() callback

components.name: Readiness Action Handlers
components.description: User-initiated deployment state actions. handleReset tears down subscriptions, handleRevert rolls back to a saved snapshot, handleSave persists the current state. templateItems.useMemo provides computed timeline data for the UI layer.

components_relations.relation: Provides processed timeline state and allOk readiness flag
